data.narrative:
Dhruva Space is a Series B frontier-tech company in Space & Aerospace founded by Sanjay Nekkanti. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-stage private companies in the sector: Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The four comparables span a valuation range of $274M to $1.3B, with a median of $700M. This median serves as the central estimate given the direct stage and sector alignment. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace public universe comprises 6 tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 45.3x price-to-sales, Dhruva Space's revenue is not disclosed, precluding meaningful multiple application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $595M (low) to $805M (high), centered at $700M. This range reflects the interquartile positioning of the peer set. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue run-rate and growth trajectory relative to Aalyria and Stoke Space at the high end and Hubble Network and Sift at the low end. The relatively wide peer dispersion ($274M–$1.3B) also indicates material heterogeneity in stage readiness or market positioning within this cohort. Additional investor data and revenue visibility would refine this estimate.
